topWords.definition: arousing anger or strong emotion

definitions.en: arousing anger or strong emotion

samples: The teacher's inflammatory remarks during the debate caused several students to feel upset and defensive about their opinions. (1601)

topWords.definition: to cause to stand motionless with awe, amazement, or some other strong emotion; to rivet

definitions.en: to cause to stand motionless with awe, amazement, or some other strong emotion; to rivet
definitions.ko:
1. [공포 따위로] ...을 오금을 못쓰게 하다
2. ...을 고정시키다, 못박다

samples: The breathtaking view of the sunset over the mountains transfixed everyone at the campsite, leaving them speechless in its beauty. (1602)

topWords.definition: a harmful action; an ill turn

definitions.en: a harmful action; an ill turn

samples: Ignoring someone's feelings can be a disservice to both the person and the relationship. (1603)

topWords.definition: to suddenly reject or abandon (a lover).

definitions.en: to suddenly reject or abandon (a lover).

samples: After years of dating, she decided to jilt him for someone who shared her dreams and ambitions. (1604)

topWords.definition: to cut or carve lines, letters, designs, etc., onto or into a hard surface

definitions.en: to cut or carve lines, letters, designs, etc., onto or into a hard surface

samples: The artist decided to engrave her name on the beautiful wooden box as a personal touch. (1605)

topWords.definition: an arbitrary decree or order

definitions.en: an arbitrary decree or order

samples: The principal issued a fiat requiring all students to wear uniforms starting next semester. (1606)

topWords.definition: having to do with an event or events that can't be explained scientifically; supernatural

definitions.en: having to do with an event or events that can't be explained scientifically; supernatural

samples: The haunted house was famous for its paranormal activities, drawing visitors eager to experience the unexplained events that occurred there. (1607)

topWords.definition: rude and disrespectful behavior.

definitions.en: rude and disrespectful behavior.

samples: Her insolence towards the teacher during class resulted in a warning and a call to her parents. (1608)

topWords.definition: very eager or curious to hear or see something.

definitions.en: very eager or curious to hear or see something.

samples: The students were agog with excitement as they waited for the science fair to begin. (1609)

topWords.definition: a quality or characteristic belonging to or associated with someone or something

definitions.en: a quality or characteristic belonging to or associated with someone or something

samples: Honesty is an important attribute that many people admire in a friend. (1610)

topWords.definition: preventing or hindering something

definitions.en: preventing or hindering something

samples: The high cost of the equipment was prohibitive, making it difficult for many schools to participate in the science fair. (1611)

topWords.definition: a feeling of fear or agitation about something that may happen.

definitions.en: a feeling of fear or agitation about something that may happen.

samples: As the storm clouds gathered overhead, a sense of trepidation filled the students waiting for the school bus. (1612)

topWords.definition: a pouring forth

definitions.en: a pouring forth
definitions.ko: 유출 (물), 스며나옴, 심정의 토로, 토로한 말 (시문) Effuse v 발산 (유출)하다 Effusive a 과장된, 쏟아져나오는, 넘치는

samples: The effusion of emotions during the graduation ceremony brought tears to many eyes as students reflected on their journey. (1613)

topWords.definition: a trick

definitions.en: a trick

samples: The magician's ruse amazed the audience as he made it seem like he had vanished into thin air. (1614)

topWords.definition: notoriety, extreme ill repute

definitions.en: notoriety, extreme ill repute

samples: The criminal's actions led to his infamy, as he became known for his terrible deeds across the entire city. (1615)

topWords.definition: a factor or element that determines an outcome

definitions.en: a factor or element that determines an outcome

samples: The weather is often a key determinant of whether we will have a picnic or stay indoors. (1616)

topWords.definition: not admitting of passage or capable of being affected

definitions.en: not admitting of passage or capable of being affected

samples: The raincoat was designed to be impervious to water, keeping the wearer dry even in the heaviest downpour. (1617)

topWords.definition: offense or annoyance.

definitions.en: offense or annoyance.

samples: She took umbrage at the rude comments made by her classmates during the discussion. (1618)

topWords.definition: to harass; to annoy

definitions.en: to harass; to annoy

samples: The constant questions from his classmates began to harry him, making it difficult for him to concentrate on his studies. (1619)

topWords.definition: yes.

definitions.en: yes.

samples: The teacher asked if everyone understood the lesson, and the students responded with a loud "yea!" (1620)

topWords.definition: to compensate for or counterbalance

definitions.en: to compensate for or counterbalance
definitions.ko: (반대의 성질의 무언가를) 추가해서 균형을 더 맞추다

samples: The team worked extra hours to offset the delays caused by the unexpected storm. (1621)

topWords.definition: to feel or express sorrow, disapproval

definitions.en: to feel or express sorrow, disapproval

samples: Many teachers deplore the lack of respect some students show towards their classmates. (1622)

topWords.definition: please

definitions.en: please

samples: Prithee, tell me what you think about the new book we read in class. (1623)

topWords.definition: strongly reminiscent or suggestive of something

definitions.en: strongly reminiscent or suggestive of something

samples: The garden was redolent with the sweet fragrance of blooming flowers, reminding everyone of the beauty of spring. (1624)

topWords.definition: to make up for something excessively, at times due to guilt or fear

definitions.en: to make up for something excessively, at times due to guilt or fear

samples: After losing the race, she tried to overcompensate by practicing even harder for the next competition. (1625)

topWords.definition: senility; foolish affection

definitions.en: senility; foolish affection

samples: As her grandmother entered her dotage, the family noticed her increasing forgetfulness and tendency to become overly attached to her childhood belongings. (1626)

topWords.definition: assumed to be true

definitions.en: assumed to be true

samples: The teacher presumed that all the students had completed their homework before the class started. (1627)

topWords.definition: arrogantly superior, disdainful

definitions.en: arrogantly superior, disdainful

samples: The haughty student looked down on her classmates, believing she was far more intelligent than they were. (1628)

topWords.definition: a person of noble birth; an aristocrat

definitions.en: a person of noble birth; an aristocrat

samples: The patrician attended the grand banquet, showcasing the elegance and traditions of his noble lineage. (1629)

topWords.definition: a continuous whole without clear division into parts

definitions.en: a continuous whole without clear division into parts

samples: The spectrum of colors in a rainbow forms a beautiful continuum, blending seamlessly from red to violet without any distinct breaks. (1630)

coreWords.definition: keeping careful watch for possible danger or difficulties

definitions.en: keeping careful watch for possible danger or difficulties

samples: The lifeguard remained vigilant as he scanned the beach for any signs of trouble among the swimmers. (1281)

coreWords.definition: appropriate; coming at the right time

definitions.en: appropriate; coming at the right time

samples: Her comment was apropos to the discussion, providing valuable insight just when we needed it. (1282)

coreWords.definition: to faint from extreme emotion.

definitions.en: to faint from extreme emotion.

samples: The crowd began to swoon as the famous singer stepped onto the stage, overwhelmed by excitement. (1283)

coreWords.definition: clever in a dishonest way

definitions.en: clever in a dishonest way

samples: The sly fox tricked the farmer into leaving the henhouse unguarded. (1284)

coreWords.definition: to invent, create falsely

definitions.en: to invent, create falsely

samples: The student tried to fabricate a story about why he was late to class, but his teacher saw through his lies. (1285)

coreWords.definition: while.

definitions.en: while.

samples: I like to listen to music whilst I study for my exams. (1286)

coreWords.definition: a feeling of doubt or hesitation with regard to the morality or propriety of a course of action.

definitions.en: a feeling of doubt or hesitation with regard to the morality or propriety of a course of action.

samples: She had scruples about accepting the reward, knowing it might encourage cheating among her classmates. (1287)

coreWords.definition: to think of (someone or something) in a particular way

definitions.en: to think of (someone or something) in a particular way

samples: The teacher will deem the students' projects successful if they meet all the requirements outlined in the rubric. (1288)

coreWords.definition: something that causes an important event to happen; (chemistry) a substance that accelerates a chemical reaction

definitions.en: something that causes an important event to happen; (chemistry) a substance that accelerates a chemical reaction

samples: The teacher's inspiring words acted as a catalyst for the students to pursue their dreams and work harder in their studies. (1289)

coreWords.definition: worthless stuff

definitions.en: worthless stuff

samples: The farmer separated the valuable grain from the chaff that was left behind after the harvest. (1290)

coreWords.definition: recovering readily from adversity

definitions.en: recovering readily from adversity

samples: The resilient athlete trained hard every day, determined to recover from her injury and compete in the championship. (1291)

coreWords.definition: to fill or pervade with a quality or substance

definitions.en: to fill or pervade with a quality or substance

samples: The teacher wanted to infuse the lesson with excitement to engage her students more effectively. (1292)

coreWords.definition:
1. to begin a journey especially on a ship or airplane
2. to make a start

definitions.en:
1. to begin a journey especially on a ship or airplane
2. to make a start

samples: The students were excited to embark on their field trip to the science museum. (1293)

coreWords.definition: genuine

definitions.en: genuine

samples: She made an earnest effort to help her classmates understand the difficult material. (1294)

coreWords.definition: easy, requiring little effort

definitions.en: easy, requiring little effort

samples: The facile solution to the math problem made it easy for all the students to complete the assignment quickly. (1295)

coreWords.definition: to be fond of; to be attached to

definitions.en: to be fond of; to be attached to

samples: I cherish the time I spend with my family during our weekend gatherings. (1296)

coreWords.definition: a refusal to obey something or someone

definitions.en: a refusal to obey something or someone

samples: The student's defiance in refusing to follow the unfair rules led to a discussion about the importance of standing up for what is right. (1297)

coreWords.definition: emotional release

definitions.en: emotional release

samples: After a long week of exams, watching a heartfelt movie provided her with the catharsis she needed to feel refreshed and ready for the challenges ahead. (1298)

coreWords.definition: causing cancer

definitions.en: causing cancer

samples: Many studies have shown that smoking cigarettes is carcinogenic and can lead to serious health issues. (1299)

coreWords.definition: diligent, thorough, and extremely attentive to details

definitions.en: diligent, thorough, and extremely attentive to details

samples: The scrupulous student double-checked her math homework to ensure that every calculation was correct. (1300)

coreWords.definition: used to indicate that something is open to debate or discussion

definitions.en: used to indicate that something is open to debate or discussion

samples: Arguably, the best time to visit the beach is during the early morning when it is less crowded. (1301)

coreWords.definition: not doing what someone or something with authority tells you to do

definitions.en: not doing what someone or something with authority tells you to do

samples: The disobedient student chose to ignore the teacher's instructions during the group project. (1302)

coreWords.definition: fair and just in the distribution of resources or opportunities

definitions.en: fair and just in the distribution of resources or opportunities

samples: The school implemented an equitable system for distributing resources to ensure every student had access to the same opportunities for learning. (1303)

coreWords.definition: absurd; ludicrous

definitions.en: absurd; ludicrous

samples: The farcical play had everyone laughing with its silly characters and outrageous situations. (1304)

coreWords.definition: to a serious examination and judgment of something

definitions.en: to a serious examination and judgment of something

samples: In her art class, Maria learned how to critique her peers' paintings by discussing the techniques and emotions they conveyed. (1305)

coreWords.definition: equality in value, status, or rank

definitions.en: equality in value, status, or rank

samples: The company strives to maintain parity among its employees by ensuring equal pay for equal work. (1306)

coreWords.definition: to limit (a claim); to specify a condition to make a claim narrower

definitions.en: to limit (a claim); to specify a condition to make a claim narrower

samples: To qualify her statement about the project, she added that the results were only applicable to the first phase of the study. (1307)

coreWords.definition: a layer; a level

definitions.en: a layer; a level

samples: The archaeologists discovered a stratum of ancient pottery buried beneath the soil, revealing insights into the lives of the people who once lived there. (1308)

coreWords.definition: capable of being shaped or molded; easily influenced or impressionable

definitions.en: capable of being shaped or molded; easily influenced or impressionable

samples: The artist appreciated how plastic the clay was, allowing her to create intricate designs with ease. (1309)

coreWords.definition: friendship

definitions.en: friendship

samples: The students developed a strong camaraderie as they worked together on their group project, supporting each other through challenges. (1310)
